lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Kick a Lucky Block Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Track code status clearly: active codes, expired entries, and whether the in-game redemption menu is currently available.

steps.title: Check Active Codes First
steps.description: Start with a simple active-code status panel. If there are no working codes, state it clearly so players do not waste attempts.

steps.title: Review Expired or Removed Codes
steps.description: Keep an expired section to prevent repeated retries on dead strings copied from old videos and repost pages.

steps.title: Confirm Whether a Code Box Exists
steps.description: If the game UI has no code-entry field, call that out directly so players understand why every pasted code fails.

steps.title: Filter Out Fake Reward Claims
steps.description: Ignore posts promising instant gems, spins, or money boosts unless they match an actual in-game redemption flow.

steps.title: Watch Official Drop Channels
steps.description: Monitor Roblox game updates and official community posts for any first-time code rollout announcement.

quickTips: No code box means no code can be redeemed yet.
Retest only when the game UI changes or an official post announces codes.
Keep active and expired lists separate to reduce confusion.
Use plain status language so players can decide fast and keep playing.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Kick a Lucky Block Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: The first-hour loop is simple: kick, land in a zone, outrun the wave, place your brainrot, and reinvest the payout.

cards.name: Kick at Your Plot
cards.description: Each run starts at your plot. Your current kick power controls how far the lucky block can travel.

cards.name: Push Into Better Zones
cards.description: Farther zones open better brainrot pools. Even small weight upgrades can move you into stronger reward bands.

cards.name: Beat the Tsunami Timer
cards.description: After every kick, return speed matters. If the wave catches you, the run does not convert into plot income.

cards.name: Spend for Momentum
cards.description: Use early cash on weights and run speed so every next run is safer, faster, and more profitable.

milestones: Reach your first consistent return route
Fill your early plot slots with stable earners
Unlock your first meaningful weight jump

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Kick a Lucky Block Brainrots
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Brainrots are your economy engine. Better zones and safer returns give better units, and better units fund faster upgrades.

items.name: Starter Brainrots
items.description: Use early drops to fill empty slots quickly. Slot uptime matters more than perfect rarity in the first phase.

items.name: Mid-Tier Brainrots
items.description: Once your route is reliable, replace weak starters with stronger mid-tier units to smooth upgrade pacing.

items.name: High-Rarity Brainrots
items.description: Mythic-and-above drops accelerate your economy and open faster access to expensive weight jumps.

items.name: Mutated Brainrots
items.description: A strong mutation can outperform a plain higher-tier unit. Compare income value before replacing.

items.name: Plot Slot Optimization
items.description: Keep your strongest earners active. Rotate out low-yield units once better zone access is stable.

items.name: Risk-Adjusted Carry Picks
items.description: During difficult wave routes, favor reliable returns over overly risky long-distance attempts.

items.name: Rebirth Prep Portfolio
items.description: Before rebirth, build a balanced set of strong earners so post-reset recovery is faster.

items.name: Endgame Keep List
items.description: Prioritize top-tier or high-multiplier units that still scale when weight and speed costs rise sharply.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Kick a Lucky Block Weights
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Weights drive kick power growth. The best route is to buy real power spikes instead of over-investing in tiny steps.

solutions.name: Wooden Stick to Bone Barbell
solutions.description: Move off the default starter quickly and secure your first meaningful power increase.

solutions.name: Stone Block to Copper Plate
solutions.description: This transition decides how soon you can consistently reach better zones and higher-value returns.

solutions.name: Iron and Ice Tier Weights
solutions.description: Mid-tier purchases should align with your run speed so longer kicks remain recoverable.

solutions.name: Donut to Endgame Plates
solutions.description: Late weights are expensive, so combine rebirth multipliers and strong plot income before committing.

managementTips: Buy for breakpoint value, not for cosmetic rarity labels.
Never let kick distance outpace your survival route.
If income stalls, improve brainrot quality before forcing another weight tier.
Tie each major weight purchase to a concrete zone target.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Kick a Lucky Block Zones
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Learn every zone from Common to Celestial and how Kick Power changes the Brainrot you roll. Higher zones give better Brainrots, but they also make the return run more dangerous.

cards.name: 1. Common (Starter)
cards.description: Kick Power: no training or very low power. Brainrot value: lowest tier. Use it only to start passive income, then move into Rare quickly after early weight upgrades.

cards.name: 2. Rare (Starter)
cards.description: Kick Power: low. Brainrot value: low to moderate. This is an early training stop, so use the income for weight upgrades and prepare for Epic.

cards.name: 3. Epic (Early Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: early upgrades such as Bone Barbell range. Brainrot value: first meaningful tier. Add some Run Speed before pushing farther to survive longer returns.

cards.name: 4. Legend (Early-Mid Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: mid power after stronger early weights. Brainrot value: stronger early-mid earnings. Start replacing weak Common and Rare units here.

cards.name: 5. Mythic (Mid Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: high. Brainrot value: major income jump. Prioritize stronger Mythic pulls and expand plot capacity so valuable rolls are not wasted.

cards.name: 6. Godly (Mid-Late Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: high with stronger weights. Brainrot value: very high, often millions per second. Pair every distance push with Speed upgrades for safer returns.

cards.name: 7. Secret (Late Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: very high. Brainrot value: massive rewards. Do not chase distance alone; only push when your speed can survive the tsunami chase.

cards.name: 8. Divine (Late Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: very high after multiple upgrades. Brainrot value: large multipliers with strong income potential. Use Divine runs to prepare endgame mutation cycles.

cards.name: 9. Hacked (Endgame)
cards.description: Kick Power: endgame. Brainrot value: top-three tier with billion-per-second potential. Time runs around luck windows to maximize mutation value.

cards.name: 10. OG (Endgame+)
cards.description: Kick Power: very high endgame. Brainrot value: billion-plus potential. Farm OG only when Run Speed can support the full return distance.

cards.name: 11. Celestial (Final Endgame)
cards.description: Kick Power: highest tier. Brainrot value: maximum listed value. Best used with stacked rebirth multipliers, strong speed, and active luck boosts.

highlights: Treat zones as a progression ladder from Common to Celestial.
Increase Kick Power only when Run Speed can still secure the return.
Use higher-zone farming to support mutation and rebirth planning.
Replace low-tier plot units aggressively as zone quality improves.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Kick a Lucky Block Mutations
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Mutations turn a normal Brainrot into a higher-earning version. Each mutation boosts both cash-per-second income and sale value, making high-zone mutated Brainrots the strongest progression targets.

regions.name: Gold (Golden)
regions.description: Increases earning rate and sale value by 1.5x. Useful early, but replace it when stronger zones and mutations become available.

regions.name: Diamond
regions.description: Doubles earning rate and sale value. Strong on Rare, Epic, and Legend while building toward deeper zones.

regions.name: Plasma
regions.description: Quadruples earning rate and sale value. A strong mid-game keeper when it lands on Mythic or better units.

regions.name: Molten
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 6x. Prioritize Molten on Godly, Secret, and Divine units for better scaling.

regions.name: Radioactive
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 8x. This is a major late-game farming spike on already valuable Brainrots.

regions.name: Shadow
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 12x. Save this tier for high-value zones because the multiplier is too strong for starter units.

regions.name: Electrified
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 16x. Extremely strong with rebirth cash multipliers in endgame loops.

regions.name: Rainbow
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 30x. The top listed mutation target for Perfect Kick runs and global luck windows.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Kick a Lucky Block Rebirth
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Rebirth trades your current Kick Power for permanent cash multipliers. It is the main long-term progression system after you start reaching better zones consistently.

creatures.name: What does Rebirth do?
creatures.description: Rebirth resets your current Kick Power progression and grants a permanent cash multiplier. Later rebirths stack so future Brainrots earn faster.

creatures.name: What is the first Rebirth requirement?
creatures.description: The first rebirth unlocks at 1,000 Kick Power and grants a permanent 2x cash multiplier when confirmed.

creatures.name: What resets when you Rebirth?
creatures.description: Kick Power progression resets, so you train again for distance. Run Speed remains a critical long-term survival upgrade for return routes.

creatures.name: What do you keep after Rebirth?
creatures.description: You keep the cash multiplier, which applies to future earnings and helps you rebuy stronger weights faster after each cycle.

creatures.name: Should you Rebirth immediately at 1,000 Kick Power?
creatures.description: Early rebirth can accelerate progression, but often it is stronger to push one or two zones deeper first so the multiplier amplifies a better income base.

creatures.name: How does the 99 Robux Rebirth Skip work?
creatures.description: Rebirth Skip lets you reset without meeting the current Kick Power requirement. It saves time, while normal progression remains the core upgrade loop.

creatures.name: What is the best Rebirth strategy?
creatures.description: Before rebirthing, fill your plot with better Brainrots, improve Run Speed, and secure stronger weights so every reset returns to high zones faster.

creatures.name: Common Rebirth mistakes
creatures.description: Avoid resetting with a weak plot, ignoring Run Speed, or over-hoarding cash instead of upgrading income and weight progression.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Kick a Lucky Block Tsunami Survival
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: After every kick, the tsunami chase decides whether your Brainrot becomes real profit or disappears. The farther your Lucky Block lands, the more important Run Speed becomes.

items.name: Kick the Lucky Block
items.description: Start each run from your plot or safe area. Landing distance determines zone quality and the Brainrot tier you can roll.

items.name: Watch the landing distance
items.description: Use the reached zone to estimate return risk. Common through Legend is easier, while Mythic and above create longer and riskier chases.

items.name: Become the Brainrot and turn back fast
items.description: Once the Brainrot appears, return toward safety immediately. Delay can waste a strong roll before it is banked.

items.name: Avoid the tsunami wave
items.description: Move directly back with minimal detours. If the wave catches you before return, the rolled Brainrot is lost.

items.name: Place the Brainrot on your plot
items.description: After reaching safety, place the unit immediately so it starts generating passive cash for progression.

items.name: Upgrade Run Speed before pushing farther
items.description: Spend income on Speed upgrades near the Weight Shop as distance grows. Higher Kick Power without matching Speed is unstable.

items.name: Balance Kick Power and Speed
items.description: Do not chase Secret, Divine, Hacked, OG, or Celestial through raw power alone. Safe conversions beat repeated high-zone losses.

items.name: Use Rebirth and mutations to reduce wasted runs
items.description: Build permanent multipliers through Rebirth and prioritize mutated high-tier rolls you can safely return with.

unlockMilestones: Pair every major distance push with a Run Speed upgrade.
Turn back immediately after each roll to reduce wave losses.
Farm high zones only when return consistency remains stable.
Use rebirth multipliers to accelerate safe Speed and weight scaling.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Kick a Lucky Block Run Speed
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Run speed is the safety valve for long kicks. If speed lags behind power, high-value runs become unstable.

sections.name: Early Run Speed Floor
sections.description: Set a minimum speed target early so normal route mistakes do not wipe your income loop.

sections.name: Mid-Game Speed Pairing
sections.description: Pair each weight jump with a speed check to keep return consistency high.

sections.name: Late-Game Recovery Pace
sections.description: At long distances, even small speed upgrades can rescue otherwise failed return windows.

sections.name: Rebirth Speed Rebuild
sections.description: After reset, recover speed quickly to avoid stalling your new progression cycle.

growthMilestones: No repeated wave catches on your core route
Speed upgrade before each major distance push
Consistent returns from your current best zone
Stable speed rebuild after each rebirth

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Kick a Lucky Block Perfect Kick
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Perfect-kick consistency is about timing discipline, not random spamming. Build repeatable habits for better distance results.

priorities.name: Watch the Timing Window
priorities.description: Focus on the release rhythm first. Reliable timing creates repeatable kick quality.

priorities.name: Use a Stable Camera Angle
priorities.description: Keeping your visual reference steady helps reduce input drift across repeated runs.

priorities.name: Sync With Return Safety
priorities.description: Perfect distance is only valuable when it still fits your tsunami recovery route.

priorities.name: Train in Short Sessions
priorities.description: Brief focused practice improves consistency faster than long unfocused grinding.

priorities.name: Record Your Best Rhythm
priorities.description: Track the timing feel that produced your most stable results and repeat it.

priorities.name: Increase Difficulty Gradually
priorities.description: Move to longer targets only after your current perfect-kick success rate is stable.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Kick a Lucky Block Luck Events
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Luck events are temporary windows. Treat them as planned farming sessions with clear priorities.

achievements.title: Route Ready
achievements.description: Prepare a dependable return lane before event start so boosted drops are not wasted.

achievements.title: Cash Buffer
achievements.description: Keep spending room ready for immediate weight and speed upgrades during boosted farming.

achievements.title: Slot Cleanup
achievements.description: Free low-value plot slots before the event so strong drops can be placed immediately.

achievements.title: Prioritize Safe Conversion
achievements.description: Choose routes that reliably convert boosted drops instead of chasing risky maximum distance every run.

achievements.title: Stack Smart Bonuses
achievements.description: Combine event luck with your existing mutation and progression strategy for stronger returns.

achievements.title: Track Value per Minute
achievements.description: Measure results by usable income gain, not just by occasional flashy drops.

achievements.title: Reinvest Quickly
achievements.description: Convert event gains into durable upgrades before momentum fades.

achievements.title: Refresh Rebirth Plan
achievements.description: Recalculate your next rebirth window after event-driven income spikes.

achievements.title: Keep New Core Units
achievements.description: Lock in your best event acquisitions as the base for standard non-event farming.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Kick a Lucky Block Game Passes
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Game passes can speed specific bottlenecks. Buy only when a pass clearly improves your current stage.

faqs.question: Which pass helps early progression the most?
faqs.answer: The best early value is usually the pass that improves your immediate cash flow or consistency, not a niche bonus you cannot leverage yet.

faqs.question: Should I buy mutation-focused passes first?
faqs.answer: Only after your core kick and return loop is stable. Mutation boosts are stronger when your base farming route already works.

faqs.question: When does x2 cash become worth it?
faqs.answer: It becomes more efficient once you already run steady returns and can continuously reinvest into power and speed upgrades.

faqs.question: Do game passes replace rebirth strategy?
faqs.answer: No. Passes accelerate parts of progression, but rebirth timing and route discipline still decide long-term scaling.

faqs.question: Can free players stay competitive without passes?
faqs.answer: Yes. A clean free-to-play route with strong timing, upgrades, and rebirth pacing can progress steadily.

faqs.question: What is the safest purchase approach?
faqs.answer: Buy one pass at a time, measure the practical gain over a few sessions, then decide whether another pass is justified.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Kick a Lucky Block AFK Income
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: AFK routines work best when your plot is already filled with efficient earners and your spending routine is disciplined.

faqs.question: How do I set up a simple AFK loop?
faqs.answer: Fill your strongest available slots, keep your route stable, then let passive income accumulate before periodic upgrade check-ins.

faqs.question: What should I buy first after an AFK session?
faqs.answer: Prioritize the upgrade that unlocks your next zone or protects return consistency, usually weight or speed.

faqs.question: Is AFK better than active farming?
faqs.answer: AFK is steady but slower. Active sessions are better for breakthrough milestones, while AFK smooths your baseline growth.

faqs.question: How often should I rotate plot units?
faqs.answer: Refresh whenever new units clearly outperform older fillers. Keep top earners locked in to protect passive output.

faqs.question: Does AFK help rebirth timing?
faqs.answer: Yes. It can close the final resource gap before rebirth, especially when you are near a planned threshold.

faqs.question: How do I avoid wasting AFK gains?
faqs.answer: Plan your next two purchases in advance so idle income is converted immediately into meaningful progress.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Kick a Lucky Block No Robux Progression
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: A no-Robux route is fully viable with disciplined upgrades, safer returns, and smart rebirth timing.

settings.name: Phase 1: Stabilize Basics
settings.description: Prioritize safe returns and quick slot filling before chasing long-distance high-variance runs.

settings.name: Phase 2: Weight Breakpoints
settings.description: Target meaningful weight jumps and avoid overpaying for upgrades that do not unlock new zones.

settings.name: Phase 3: Speed Insurance
settings.description: Upgrade run speed whenever longer routes start failing, even if power upgrades are available.

settings.name: Phase 4: Plot Quality Shift
settings.description: Replace low-yield units methodically so passive income scales without premium boosts.

settings.name: Phase 5: Event Window Farming
settings.description: Use luck events as free acceleration windows and reinvest gains immediately.

settings.name: Phase 6: Rebirth Cadence
settings.description: Rebirth on milestone logic, then rebuild quickly using your tested route template.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Kick a Lucky Block Updates
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Use update notes to adjust priorities fast: new multipliers, economy tweaks, and event windows can change optimal routes.

entries.title: Track Economy and Drop Table Changes
entries.description: When reward balance changes, re-evaluate your best zone target and replacement threshold immediately.

entries.title: Prioritize Limited-Time Luck Windows
entries.description: Temporary boosts can outperform normal farming; shift your session plan around active events.

entries.title: Review Any Rebirth Formula Adjustments
entries.description: If reset values or multipliers move, update your rebirth timing checklist before the next cycle.

entries.title: Recheck Weight and Speed Breakpoints
entries.description: Balance updates can make previously weak upgrade paths suddenly efficient.

entries.title: Adopt New UI or Flow Improvements
entries.description: Small interface upgrades often reduce downtime and improve session consistency.

entries.title: Refresh Your Module Priorities
entries.description: After each significant update, reorder your focus across codes, farming, and rebirth planning.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Kick a Lucky Block Live Stats and Official Links
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Use live metrics and official destinations to verify you are tracking the right game and the right community channels.

steps.title: Confirm Official Roblox Experience
steps.description: Open the official Roblox game page first to avoid lookalike entries or outdated mirrors.

steps.title: Check Current Player Activity
steps.description: Active player trends help you spot peak windows for events and social updates.

steps.title: Monitor Visit and Favorite Growth
steps.description: Visit and favorite movement is useful for understanding update impact over time.

steps.title: Use Official Community Channels
steps.description: Follow the official Discord and developer group for announcements, events, and community notices.

steps.title: Bookmark Trusted External Destinations
steps.description: Keep quick access to Roblox, Discord, YouTube gameplay references, and the developer group.

steps.title: Revalidate Links After Major Updates
steps.description: When update cycles are active, quickly verify your source links are still current and relevant.

faq.title: Kick a Lucky Block
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for code status, progression choices, and update tracking.

questions.question: Are there active codes right now?
questions.answer: The code status can change over time, so the fastest check is the homepage code module plus official game announcements.

questions.question: What should I upgrade first: weight or run speed?
questions.answer: If your returns are failing, prioritize speed. If your routes are stable, prioritize weight to unlock better zones.

questions.question: When should I rebirth?
questions.answer: Rebirth when your current route is stable and your reset multiplier will clearly accelerate the next cycle.

questions.question: How do I farm better brainrots consistently?
questions.answer: Push distance gradually, keep return safety high, and replace low-yield units as soon as stronger options stabilize.

questions.question: Do I need game passes to progress well?
questions.answer: No. A disciplined free-to-play path with clean upgrade timing can progress steadily through all major systems.

questions.question: Where should I follow updates?
questions.answer: Use the official Roblox page, developer group, and Discord community for the most direct update flow.
